depression.description: I feel completely overwhelmed with worry about my father's health and how it affects our family dynamics. It's hard to focus on anything else.
I often feel helpless and powerless when it comes to my father's health issues. It seems like no matter what I do, I can't change the situation.
I'm scared that if my father passes away, I'll lose my connection with my family. It feels like everything is falling apart.
When I'm feeling down, I tend to isolate myself. I can't seem to reach out to anyone for support, even though I know I need it.

Delusion.description: I struggle to trust that my family will communicate openly with me. It feels like they withhold important information, and I can't rely on them.
I feel a deep resentment towards my father for being emotionally distant. It makes it hard for me to connect with him, especially during such a difficult time.
I feel like it's my job to keep the peace in the family, but it weighs heavily on me. I often worry that if I don't manage things, everything will fall apart.

psychosis.description: I find myself constantly ruminating about my father's mortality. The thought of losing him is terrifying, and I don't know how to cope with it.
I struggle to bring up difficult topics with my family, especially about health issues. I feel paralyzed by fear of how they might react.
